steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank is the verb for the subject even-toed ungulates. The phrase "such as the domestic goat (Capra aegagrus hircus)" is a parenthetical example set off by commas — strip it out and the sentence reads: "even-toed ungulates ______ to a vast and diverse order of animals." The subject is plural, so the verb must agree.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — belongs: third-person singular (the -s ending marks singular agreement). "Even-toed ungulates belongs" is a subject-verb mismatch. ✗
  • Choice B — has belonged: the auxiliary "has" is singular. Same number error as A, plus present-perfect aspect is unwarranted here — the sentence states a permanent taxonomic fact. ✗
  • Choice C — is belonging: the auxiliary "is" is singular, creating another number error. Progressive aspect is also wrong for a fixed, ongoing classification. ✗
  • Choice D — belong: base form with no inflection — correct for a plural subject. "Even-toed ungulates belong to a vast and diverse order" is grammatical. ✓
That matches Choice D — belong.
